OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Dec-19-2018 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-13-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Douglas Akay, Esq. 415-764-1999 of Akay Law for Plaintiff.
Jeffrey Rosenfeld, Esq. 424-239-3417 of BlankRome for Defendant HARRY WINSTON, INC..

RULING - A R G U E D. Counsel for Weber Electric Argues and cites case authority. The matter is continued to be heard at 2pm for the Court to review case authority provided. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT/CROSS-CLAIM DEFENDANT HARRY WINSTON, INC.'S (FROM CASE CGC-18-565880) DEMURRER TO THE SIXTH, EIGHTH, NINTH, TENTH, ELEVENTH, AND TWELFTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT FILED BY E.J. WEBER ELECTRIC COMPNAY, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Cross-defendant Harry Winston, Inc.'s demurrer to the first amended cross-complaint filed by E.J. Weber Electric Company, Inc. is sustained without leave to amend as to the eighth and ninth causes of action for intentional interference with contract and overruled as to the sixth tenth, eleventh and twelfth causes of action for negligence, declaratory relief, equitable indemnity and comparative indemnity. Because HWI (as the lessee and beneficiary of all the construction and improvement work) is a third beneficiary of the contracts it allegedly interfered with, HWI is not a stranger to those contracts and cannot be liable for interfering with those contracts. While the negligence claim has some attributes similar to a nonactionable claim for negligent interference with contract, liberally construed the negligence claim is based on a legal duty separate from a duty not to interfere with Weber Electric's contracts. Weber Electric has adequately alleged a legal duty sounding in negligence on the part of HWI not to take any action or refrain from taking any action that would cause foreseeable harm to Weber Electric. The declaratory relief claim is adequately alleged since there is a current dispute regarding the responsibility for the damages claimed by Build Group, Inc. The negligence claim is a sufficient predicate for the indemnity claims. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
